advantages: Turns O(n²) double-loops on sequences into O(n) algorithms.
Uses O(1) or O(k) extra memory — friendly to streams and large inputs.
Same skeleton solves a wide range of contiguous-sub-array problems.
Stable index-based logic — easy to reason about and unit-test.
disadvantages: Only applies to *sequential* data with O(1) indexed access (not great for general graphs).
Many problems look 'window-like' but don't have the required monotonic validity (need DP).
Off-by-one mistakes on bounds are common; bugs are subtle and pass naive tests.
Sliding-window for max/min requires extra structure (monotonic deque) — a step up in complexity.
